proofVocabulary.meaning: A deterministic service or state fixture passes.
Ordinary engine entities and callbacks pass.
A new process restores the exact scenario.
Packaged server, clients, reconnect, and late join converge.
Behavior and performance remain bounded over the declared duration and load.
Every required lower proof rung passes on one unchanged package.

contractRows.domain: Setup, faction choice, campaign slots, parameters, solo and co-op
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.0 added true single-player behavior; CE 3.11.1 is the pinned baseline.
contractRows.observableContract: A new campaign exposes explicit faction, parameter, commander, membership, solo/co-op, and starting-position rules before durable play begins.
contractRows.formulaContract: Starting resources, player scaling, initial ownership, and setup restrictions must be recorded as pinned data rather than inferred from labels.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Use Reforger lobby, role, and pause capabilities; any missing single-player time-control behavior must be a visible scope decision.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState setup, player, faction, HQ, and preset fields
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Setup creates only the authored/runtime entities owned by the accepted campaign choices.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: The strategic graph exists before any render-bubble projection.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Starting balances and grants are typed once-only bootstrap facts.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Setup completion, chosen factions, parameters, and identities survive restart without replay.
contractRows.clientProjection: Every client and late joiner sees the same setup completion, commander, factions, and starting state.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 8 resistance progression and Gate 10 complete-campaign validation

contractRows.domain: HQ, Petros, commander, membership, rank, points, personal and faction money
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.1 retains the CE 3.11.x commander, HQ, Petros, and split player/faction economy model.
contractRows.observableContract: Players can distinguish personal authority and money from faction authority and money, while HQ and Petros create meaningful campaign risk.
contractRows.formulaContract: Rank, points, election, income, HQ discovery, Petros loss, and rebuild values require golden CE 3.11.1 fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Backend identities and Reforger role/locality rules replace Arma 3 player-object assumptions.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState player, commander, economy, HQ, and Petros records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: HQ assets and Petros are native projections with exact ownership and lifecycle callbacks.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: HQ knowledge, threat, attack preparation, and recovery continue without nearby players.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Personal and faction mutations require separate stable ledger identities and once-only settlement.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Identity, membership, balances, HQ threat, Petros state, and pending consequences restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Authorized clients receive clear commander/member state, balances, HQ threat, and disabled reasons.

contractRows.domain: Location taxonomy, map graph, ownership, flags, garrisons, and facilities
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.0 expanded invader town capture and persistent strategic assets.
contractRows.observableContract: Every strategic location has one stable identity and one owner; military sites change through physical control and publish every consequence once.
contractRows.formulaContract: Location values, capture conditions, garrison policy, and war-level contribution must match the pinned rules or an approved adaptation.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Everon authored locations and Game Master markers replace Arma 3 marker topology while retaining the same strategic cause and effect.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState zone, route, garrison, ownership receipt, and strategic-event records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Native defenders, flags, authored markers, vehicles, and assets follow one accepted transition revision.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Off-screen ownership, garrison policy, and strategic consequences use the same revisioned receipt.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Capture rewards, losses, and enemy commitments settle once from the transition receipt.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Owner, revision, pending transition, garrison policy, and legacy location aliases restore without duplicate side effects.
contractRows.clientProjection: Host, clients, reconnect, and JIP converge on authored and projected owner state.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 5 strategic truth and living Everon

contractRows.domain: Town population, three-way support, political flips, HR, tax, and police
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.x retains political town capture and enemy-town police; invader town capture is part of the current baseline.
contractRows.observableContract: Towns change side through population support rather than a generic military hold, and enemy police retire when resistance ownership is accepted.
contractRows.formulaContract: Population, faction support, event magnitude/expiry, flip threshold, HR, and tax formulas require golden fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: The current policy intentionally creates no automatic resistance police; resistance security must come from explicit garrison policy unless a divergence is approved.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState town influence, civilian consequence, local-security, zone, and ownership records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Town civilians and enemy local security move, react, suffer exact casualties, and retire on owner change.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Support events, income, political flips, and security epochs advance without player proximity.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Aid, casualties, missions, taxes, HR, and ownership consequences settle once through their domain authorities.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Population, three-way support, event history, epoch, casualties, and owner transition restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Map/War rows, town details, markers, and notifications use the same support and ownership revisions.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 5 strategic truth and living Everon

contractRows.domain: Resources, factories, seaports, airfields, radio sites, banks, and roadblocks
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.0 added or expanded persistent strategic assets, logistics distinctions, and garage/economy behavior.
contractRows.observableContract: Each facility has a distinct strategic value, lifecycle, income/logistics consequence, and enemy response rather than acting as a generic capture-progress source.
contractRows.formulaContract: Income multipliers, airfield restrictions, radio effects, roadblock effects, and facility-specific rewards require pinned fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Only stock Reforger assets with a safe native lifecycle are enabled; unavailable strategic asset families are explicit deferrals.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState zones, facilities, radio sites, strategic events, and economy records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Facility assets bind to stable authored/generated entities and accept native destruction, repair, capture, and construction evidence.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Income, rebuild, radio, and roadblock effects continue from durable strategic state.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Every income, rebuild, purchase, loss, and mission effect has a typed once-only transaction.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Facility lifecycle, asset identity, destruction epoch, and pending settlement restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Facility state, task, marker, report, and disabled reason converge for all clients.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 7 mission parity and Gate 8 progression

contractRows.domain: War level, aggression, attack/support resources, planning, QRFs, counterattacks, and reinforcements
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.0 made counterattacks budget-backed and reinforcements physically interceptable; CE 3.11.1 is the pinned correction level.
contractRows.observableContract: Occupier and invader plan and spend separately, fight each other and the resistance, and continue campaigning off-screen.
contractRows.formulaContract: War-level thresholds, aggression, budget cadence, target choice, composition, attack timing, debit, refund, and settlement require golden fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger strategic simulation owns off-screen movement; native groups are projections of budget-backed orders.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState enemy resource pools, planning receipts, orders, operations, routes, and settlements
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Budget-backed forces materialize exact rosters and reconcile native casualties and outcomes.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Planning, travel, combat, rebuilding, and return advance with no nearby player.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Commit, debit, cancellation, survivor settlement, and refund share stable order and transaction identities.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Pools, aggression, planning cadence, commitments, routes, casualties, and receipts restore without replay.
contractRows.clientProjection: Players see only authorized intel, but operation markers/tasks remain revision-consistent across clients.

contractRows.domain: Resistance squads, garrisons, training, supports, recall, statics, and construction
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.x includes persistent resistance forces, training, high command, support restrictions, and recent rebel-aircraft behavior.
contractRows.observableContract: One accepted quote produces one immutable roster and exact cost; survivors retain duty, route, casualties, marker count, recall, and refund across projection and restart.
contractRows.formulaContract: Composition, training cap, cost, ETA, support cooldown, recall, and refund formulas require pinned fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Native AI movement and high-command UI may differ, but strategic assignment and exact force accounting may not.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState force manifests, quotes, spawn results, operations, routes, groups, supports, garrisons, and ledger rows
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: HST_ForcePlanningService, HST_OperationService, HST_SupportRequestService, and family operation services
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Exact living slots materialize once, run a bounded route, react to engagement, resume assignment, and retire on proven last death.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Duty, route, contact, casualty, return, recall, and settlement advance independent of player proximity.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Quote, reserve, commit, cancel, failure, casualty, recall, return, and archive share stable identities and exact ceilings.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Every supported force restores one manifest, living roster, assignment, route cursor, projection owner, marker, and settlement owner.
contractRows.clientProjection: UI quantity/cost/ETA, marker living count, notification, recall choice, and disabled reason derive from the authoritative records.

contractRows.domain: Mission selection, shared lifecycle, tasking, expiry, cleanup, and consequences
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.1 mission behavior includes fixes inherited from 3.11.0 and 3.11.1; category labels alone are not parity.
contractRows.observableContract: Every enabled mission has its own objective branches, assets, AI behavior, success, failure, expiry, cleanup, and strategic consequences.
contractRows.formulaContract: Mission selection, difficulty, duration, rewards, penalties, aggression, support, attack timing, and player scaling are pinned per mission.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Mission presentation and native adapter may change for Everon, while the strategic causal graph remains equivalent.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState mission, objective, asset, task, marker, event, and transaction records
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: HST_MissionService, HST_MissionRuntimeService, and mission-specific operation/lifecycle services
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Required native assets and actors exist, behave, and publish authoritative evidence without synthetic completion.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Eligible missions and traveling forces progress off-screen where the upstream behavior permits it.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Success, each failure branch, expiry, salvage, and delayed outcomes settle once through typed authorities.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Every nonterminal phase, asset binding, consequence window, and task state survives restart.
contractRows.clientProjection: Tasks, markers, briefings, progress, outcomes, and JIP snapshots agree for every client.

contractRows.domain: Assassination missions
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Pinned to CE 3.11.1 mission definitions and consequences.
contractRows.observableContract: Officer, traitor, and special-operations targets have distinct admission, guards, success/failure, escape/expiry, and strategic effects.
contractRows.formulaContract: Rewards, aggression, HQ knowledge, points, and target/guard difficulty are mission-specific.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Stock characters and Everon sites replace unavailable content while retaining distinct targets and consequences.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Mission, HVT asset, exact guard operation, consequence, and settlement records
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: HST_MissionRuntimeService and assassination guard operation services
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Separate HVT and exact guard projections use native death/deletion authority.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Expiry and strategic state continue while off-screen; the HVT is never duplicated by projection.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Each target outcome settles one mission-specific reward or penalty.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: HVT, guard roster, expiry, and consequence restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Target task and marker state converge through JIP and restart.

contractRows.domain: Conquest and facility-acquisition missions
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Pinned to CE 3.11.1 location and mission behavior.
contractRows.observableContract: Town politics and military-site control remain distinct; a generic clear-and-hold reward cannot substitute for facility-specific strategic behavior.
contractRows.formulaContract: Admission, war-level restriction, defense, hold, ownership, reward, aggression, and facility consequences are location-specific.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Everon site geometry changes physical objectives without merging political and military capture rules.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Mission, objective, combat-presence, ownership-transition, facility, and settlement records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Conscious hostile presence and valid control conditions drive the objective.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Ownership is never granted by player-distance suspension or an empty asset.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: The canonical ownership receipt owns rewards and strategic side effects once.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Hold progress, presence state, pending owner transition, and consequence restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Objective, owner, authored marker, projected marker, and Map/War row share one revision.

contractRows.domain: Money, reinforcement, supply, ammo, prisoner, and armored convoys
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.1 includes convoy server-execution corrections; CE 3.11.0 requires real interceptable reinforcements and distinct logistics cargo.
contractRows.observableContract: A convoy moves without nearby players, materializes exact vehicles/crew/cargo, can be intercepted, and settles its mission-specific payload and strategic effects.
contractRows.formulaContract: Composition, route, difficulty, money/cargo/captive outcome, attack timing, support, aggression, points, and failure are convoy-specific.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Server-local Reforger drivers, seats, road routes, and obstruction recovery replace Arma 3 vehicle-locality technique.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Mission convoy operation, route, manifest, vehicles, groups, cargo/captive, objective, and settlement records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Exact vehicles, drivers, seats, spacing, movement, contact, salvage, and cleanup use native evidence.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Staging and route progress continue with no client nearby and reconcile with physical progress.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Mission-specific payload, reward, failure, aggression, resource, and salvage outcomes settle once.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Every staging, virtual, materializing, physical, contact, folding, arrival, and settlement phase restores exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Task, convoy marker, route status, asset outcome, and JIP/restart state converge.

contractRows.domain: Destroy, sabotage, radio, cache, and armor missions
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.x retains mission-specific radio, armor, and sabotage consequences rather than one generic destroy reward.
contractRows.observableContract: The intended physical asset must be destroyed or captured through its declared branch, with mission-specific strategic effects and no ambient-equipment false positive.
contractRows.formulaContract: Radio timing/aggression, armor branch rewards/penalties, facility sabotage, and failure consequences are distinct.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Stock destructible assets and explicit damage-authority adapters replace unavailable target classes.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Mission target assets, radio lifecycle, damage witnesses, vehicle capture, objectives, and settlements
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Native damage/destruction/capture evidence binds to the intended stable asset.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Expiry and strategic rebuild state continue off-screen without inventing destruction.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Success, capture alternative, failure, expiry, and strategic effects settle once per target epoch.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Target binding, damage evidence, radio epoch, branch, and consequence restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Task, marker, target status, branch, and result converge after JIP/restart.

contractRows.domain: Bank, cache, factory, seaport, airfield, ammo-truck, and weapons-truck logistics
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.0 distinguishes weapons and ammo logistics and expands vehicle-ammo economy.
contractRows.observableContract: Cargo, vehicle, source, delivery, interception, success, failure, and strategic effect differ by logistics mission.
contractRows.formulaContract: Cargo counts, rewards, support shifts, aggression, unlocks, ammo/fuel/repair effects, and failure penalties are mission-specific.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger inventory and vehicle storage APIs replace Arma 3 cargo handling while retaining finite, typed quantities.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Mission assets, cargo ownership, carrier binding, vehicle state, arsenal/logistics, objectives, and settlement records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Exact cargo or vehicle is picked up, carried, delivered, lost, or captured through native interaction.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Mission expiry and enemy strategic consequences continue off-screen; player-bound cargo does not teleport.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Cargo transfer, rewards, penalties, unlocks, and strategic effects settle once.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Cargo, carrier, vehicle, delivery, expiry, and transaction state restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Task, asset marker, carried/delivered status, and outcome converge for clients/JIP.

contractRows.domain: POW and refugee rescue missions
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Pinned to CE 3.11.1 per-captive rescue behavior and consequences.
contractRows.observableContract: Each captive has an exact identity and lifecycle from guard state through release, following/boarding, extraction, loss, reward, and cleanup.
contractRows.formulaContract: Money, HR, town support, aggression, points, and partial/failure outcome scale per rescued or lost captive where the baseline requires it.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger following, compartment seating, and ownership callbacks replace Arma 3 captive locality behavior.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Mission captive assets, exact guard operation, follow/seat bindings, extraction, objectives, and settlements
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Captives and guards use exact native identities, seating/following, death/deletion, and extraction evidence.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Guards remain strategic projections; player-bound freed captives follow an explicit off-screen policy.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Per-captive and mission terminal outcomes settle once without fixed-count invention.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Captive status, carrier/seat, guard roster, extraction, and reward window restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Captive count, state, task, marker, and outcome converge after reconnect/JIP/restart.

contractRows.domain: Defend Petros, tower rebuild, city battle, minor task, and gun-shop missions
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Pinned to CE 3.11.1 dynamic-event behavior and inherited 3.11.x campaign pressure rules.
contractRows.observableContract: Each dynamic event is triggered by real campaign state, persists its nonterminal phases, and applies its distinct success/failure consequence.
contractRows.formulaContract: Trigger chance, HQ knowledge, timing, composition, shop inventory/prices, support, aggression, and loss consequences are event-specific.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Everon sites and Reforger interaction UI adapt presentation without replacing campaign causality with admin or synthetic timers.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Dynamic mission, HQ/Petros, radio rebuild, town battle, gun-shop inventory, objective, and transaction records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Required attackers, defenders, targets, sellers, inventory, and interactions are native and authoritative.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Threat, admission, expiry, travel, and strategic consequences continue off-screen where applicable.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Event admission, purchase, reward, penalty, and cleanup settle exactly once.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Trigger, inventory, objective, runtime binding, and consequence windows restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Tasks, shop actions, markers, HQ threat, and outcomes converge for clients/JIP.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 7 Tier A and Tier B missions

contractRows.domain: City supplies and support missions
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Pinned to CE 3.11.1 support-mission political and aggression consequences.
contractRows.observableContract: Supplies reach a contacted town through a real delivery lifecycle and change the intended political/aggression state rather than acting as generic cash.
contractRows.formulaContract: Cargo, town support shifts, enemy support shifts, aggression, success, and failure are pinned to the baseline.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Everon delivery sites and Reforger inventory interactions adapt the physical layer only.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Mission cargo, town influence, civilian consequence, objective, and settlement records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Exact supplies are collected and delivered to the target town.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Expiry and political consequence timing continue without player proximity; cargo does not teleport.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Delivery or failure publishes one political/aggression settlement and any approved economy entry.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Cargo, target town, delivery, expiry, and political effect restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Task, cargo, town, support change, and result converge after JIP/restart.

contractRows.domain: Civilians, traffic, theft, casualties, undercover, wanted state, police, and roadblocks
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.1 includes civilian-car locality corrections and retains police/undercover rules.
contractRows.observableContract: Towns feel alive while bounded civilians and traffic move, react, and affect politics; undercover status changes from gear, weapons, vehicles, roads, police, heat, and actions.
contractRows.formulaContract: Actor budgets, support consequences, wanted/heat timing, scans, detection, theft, casualties, and roadblock rules require pinned fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger AI, vehicle seats, road navigation, and perception replace Arma 3 locality and scan mechanics.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState civilian zones, actor slots, consequence receipts, undercover, heat, security, and town influence
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Pedestrians and traffic walk/drive, panic/recover, respect ownership, and publish exact theft/casualty/security evidence.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Budgets, rotation, heat, political effects, and security epochs persist without native actor leakage.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Aid, theft, casualty, scan, exposure, and political consequences settle once.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Actor slots, consequence receipts, heat, wanted state, security casualties, and town effects restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Undercover state, reasons, town support, security, and notifications agree for owner and observers as authorized.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 5 strategic truth and living Everon

contractRows.domain: Intel, reveal, interrogation, informants, prisoners, radio, and intelligence network
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.1 retains intelligence-driven reveal, radio, prisoner, and HQ knowledge systems.
contractRows.observableContract: Players gain imperfect, durable intelligence through intended actions; it reveals appropriate locations/operations and affects HQ/radio pressure.
contractRows.formulaContract: Intel chance, amount, reveal scope/duration, interrogation/informant effects, radio modifiers, and HQ knowledge require pinned fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger task and marker projection present intel while server authority retains hidden truth.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Intel, reveal, radio, prisoner, HQ knowledge, marker, and event records
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: HST_MissionService, HST_RadioSiteLifecycleService, and future dedicated intel authority
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Intel objects, prisoners, radios, and interactions publish server-authoritative evidence.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Reveal expiry, radio effects, and enemy knowledge continue from strategic time.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Intel grant, spend, reveal, interrogation, and consequence require stable once-only identities.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Intel balances, reveals, radio state, prisoners, and knowledge restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Only authorized intel is projected, with consistent markers/tasks after reconnect/JIP.

contractRows.domain: Arsenal, unlocks, loot, loadouts, garage, vehicle ammo, fuel, repair, salvage, and cargo
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.0 expanded vehicle-ammo economy, distinct logistics trucks, persistent strategic assets, and garage combat lock.
contractRows.observableContract: Loot and finite quantities drive unlocks and AI equipment; vehicles and cargo retain exact capacity, damage, fuel, ammo, attachments, ownership, storage, salvage, and combat restrictions.
contractRows.formulaContract: Unlock thresholds, capacity, garage slots, purchase/sale, combat lock, ammo/fuel/repair, salvage, and training-loadout rules require golden fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger inventory, loadout, replication, damage, fuel, turret, and compartment APIs replace Arma 3 storage mechanics.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Arsenal items, vehicle cargo, garage/runtime/field vehicles, loadouts, transactions, and mission cargo records
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: HST_ArsenalService, HST_LoadoutEditorService, and vehicle persistence/logistics services
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Native items, cargo, vehicles, attachments, fuel, damage, ammo, crew, and interaction map to exact durable identities.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Stored and off-screen vehicles/cargo retain strategic state without duplicate native roots.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Loot, withdraw, purchase, sale, capture, storage, redeploy, repair, rearm, fuel, and salvage use typed once-only mutations.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Items, quantities, unlocks, loadouts, vehicles, cargo, damage/fuel/ammo, and bindings restore exactly.
contractRows.clientProjection: Arsenal, loadout, garage, cargo, costs, capacity, and disabled reasons converge for clients.

contractRows.domain: Respawn, incapacitation, revive, death penalties, and fast travel
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Pinned to CE 3.11.1 player lifecycle and travel restrictions.
contractRows.observableContract: Death, incapacitation, revive, equipment/economy penalty, respawn choice, and fast travel are predictable and cannot bypass combat or operations.
contractRows.formulaContract: Timers, penalties, eligibility, destination control, combat distance, operation restrictions, and player scaling require pinned fixtures.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger character lifecycle and deployment UI replace Arma 3 respawn mechanics.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Player lifecycle, incapacitation, penalty, spawn, location control, combat presence, and travel request records
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: HST_PlayerSpawnService, HST_PlayerLifecycleService, and future travel authority
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Native death/revive/respawn and travel placement are server-authoritative and safe.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Eligibility and penalties use durable strategic state rather than client presence.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Death penalties and travel costs settle once per accepted lifecycle transition.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Pending incapacitation, penalty, respawn, and travel requests restore or fail safely.
contractRows.clientProjection: Players receive exact eligibility, cost, destination, denial, and lifecycle state.

contractRows.domain: Save, restart, migration, reconnect, JIP, admin controls, security, backup, and rollback
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.1 remains a persistent multiplayer campaign baseline; Partisan adds explicit recovery and authority requirements for Reforger.
contractRows.observableContract: Save/restart, reconnect, late join, duplicate request, fault recovery, backup, upgrade, and rollback never invent, duplicate, refill, lose, or replay campaign facts.
contractRows.formulaContract: Schema support windows, checkpoint cadence, history bounds, idempotency horizon, timeout, retry, and retention are explicit contracts.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger persistence callbacks and RPC ownership are wrapped by server authority, a two-generation journal, and fail-closed source selection.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: HST_CampaignState and HST_CampaignSaveData plus journal, command receipt, projection epoch, and migration records
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Native runtime is quiesced, captured, restored, rebound, or safely rejected around checkpoints and process changes.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: The full strategic graph serializes as one coherent authority snapshot.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Commands and settlements retain durable idempotency beyond detailed-history compaction.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: A declared public schema window has golden saves; unsupported or conflicting state fails with a safe actionable reason.
contractRows.clientProjection: Clients resynchronize revisioned state after join, reconnect, restore, packet gaps, and stale epochs.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 9 full graph persistence and fault tolerance

contractRows.domain: Victory, loss, pacing, difficulty, and player-count scaling
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Pinned to CE 3.11.1 campaign completion and 3.11.x economy/pacing behavior.
contractRows.observableContract: A weak resistance can progress through meaningful scarcity to a legitimate victory or loss without admin mutation, across declared player counts and difficulties.
contractRows.formulaContract: Income, HR, support, war level, aggression, mission cadence, enemy spend, training, unlocks, casualties, victory, loss, and player scaling require completed-campaign evidence.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Everon geography and available stock content may alter pacing inputs, with every tolerance or divergence recorded.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Campaign phase/end, ownership, town, economy, war level, enemy, progression, and event records
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: HST_StrategicService and the authoritative domain services feeding campaign outcome
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Natural play outcomes, not admin commands or synthetic timers, produce the strategic state used for completion.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Enemy and economy cadence continue off-screen so completion does not depend on player proximity.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Every progression and end-state mutation has a traceable typed source and once-only settlement.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Long campaigns, completion, loss, and post-end state restore without reopening or replay.
contractRows.clientProjection: All clients see the same progression, pacing read model, victory/loss transition, and final summary.

contractRows.domain: Menus, map targeting, markers, tasks, reports, notifications, and rendered accessibility
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: CE 3.11.1 supplies the player-observable behavior; Reforger requires a native UI and revisioned projection adaptation.
contractRows.observableContract: Every enabled action works end to end or has a precise disabled reason; modal input is atomic; every client sees the same authoritative campaign read model.
contractRows.formulaContract: Capacity, priority, pagination, text length, resolution, localization, input-state, revision, ACK, gap, and resync behavior are explicit.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Reforger widgets, input contexts, Game Master markers, RPCs, and client-native map markers implement the presentation layer.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: Server read models, marker records, projection epochs/revisions, tasks, notifications, and command receipts
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Rendered layouts, pointer/keyboard input, authored markers, and native client markers are exercised in ordinary packaged clients.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: The server read model remains authoritative regardless of whether a client UI is open.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: UI retries and duplicate activations cannot reapply a command; presentation text never decides command success.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Projection epochs and durable read-model sources restore; clients resnapshot rather than persisting stale local truth.
contractRows.clientProjection: Host, two clients, reconnect, JIP, missing/duplicate/delayed/reordered packets, capacity, and restart converge.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 2 immediate regressions and Gate 4 one campaign view

contractRows.domain: Development proofs, Campaign Debug, destructive admin diagnostics, and release-surface separation
contractRows.upstreamChangeHistory: Partisan-specific release engineering control; no upstream gameplay parity claim.
contractRows.observableContract: Development fixtures remain selectable and retained for engineering, while the release package exposes only safe production observability and authorized administration.
contractRows.formulaContract: Release allow/deny symbol lists, command permissions, profile isolation, timeouts, cleanup, and evidence retention are machine-validated.
contractRows.reforgerAdaptation: Use separate project/package boundaries or compile guards supported by the Reforger toolchain.
contractRows.partisanStateOwner: No production gameplay state owner; diagnostic state is disposable and explicitly scoped.
contractRows.partisanServiceOwner: Development addon/test runners and release-package audit
contractRows.physicalBehavior: Diagnostics may create native fixtures only inside owned disposable scopes with exact cleanup.
contractRows.virtualBehavior: Synthetic state never becomes ordinary campaign authority.
contractRows.transactionPolicy: Destructive or mutating diagnostics require trusted admin scope and cannot ship as ordinary player actions.
contractRows.persistenceMigration: Development artifacts are not accepted as public campaign state unless a production migration explicitly owns them.
contractRows.clientProjection: No development-only action or fixture is visible in the release client.
contractRows.blockingMilestone: Gate 1 immutable package and PR 3 development/release separation

convoy_money.gap: Current configured fixed money/town-support reward does not match the pinned money-convoy economy, attack-delay, aggression, and point consequences.

rescue_pows.gap: Exact captive/guard authority exists, but the configured fixed three-captive reward does not yet implement per-captive CE 3.11.1 scaling and full consequences.

destroy_radio_tower.gap: A narrow exact radio lifecycle exists, but the configured generic cash/capture reward does not yet reproduce the pinned radio attack-timing and aggression purpose.

destroy_or_steal_armor.gap: The current generic destroy runtime and fixed reward do not yet prove distinct steal/destroy branches, support, attack timing, aggression, points, and failure penalties.

support_city_supplies.gap: The current fixed cash/support configuration does not yet prove the pinned three-way support and aggression consequences or full failure branch.

assassinate_officer.gap: Exact guard authority is narrow; mission-specific native, restart, MP/JIP, points, and strategic consequence proof remains open.

assassinate_traitor.gap: Exact guard authority is narrow; escape/failure-to-HQ-pressure behavior and full proof ladder remain open.

assassinate_specops.gap: Exact guard authority is narrow; complete mission-specific formulas and full proof ladder remain open.

dynamic_stop_tower_rebuild.gap: A focused same-epoch radio rebuild cut exists; ordinary packaged admission, full consequences, restart, and MP/JIP remain open.

dynamic_gun_shop.gap: A bounded shop/inventory path exists, but CE 3.11.1 formula crosswalk and packaged persistence/client proof remain open.
